title: Plugin-quality LLM-judge verdict
description: The JSON contract the judged model must emit. The harness — not the model — decides pass/fail by comparing each score against the dimension floor in rubrics.py. This schema is the human-readable contract; it is NOT loaded at runtime. The runtime validator (judge.py validate_verdict) is STRICTER: beyond the constraints here it enforces the EXACT set of dimension ids applicable to the specific artifact kind (which varies by kind, so this schema stays permissive on WHICH J-ids appear and only constrains their NAME shape and value type), rejecting both missing and extra/hallucinated dimensions.

dimensions.description: Map of dimension id (J1..J11) to its score. The exact applicable subset is artifact-kind-dependent and enforced by the runtime validator, not here.

propertyNames.description: Dimension ids are J followed by one or more digits (J1..J11).

score.description: Integer 1-5 (1 = fails the dimension badly, 5 = exemplary). The JSON-schema integer type rejects booleans (true/false), matching the runtime validator which also rejects bool scores.

evidence.description: Concrete quote or specific reason for the score. No vague praise.
